title: Partners
content:
Love took chambers on our street
    Opposite to mine;
    On his door he tacked a neat,
    Clearly lettered sign.

    Straightway grew his custom great,
    For his sign read so:
    �Hearts united while you wait.
    Step in. Love and Co.�

    Much I wondered who was �Co.�
    In Love�s partnership;
    Thought across the street I�d go,
    Learn from Love�s own lip.

    So I went; and since that day
    Life is hard for me.
    I was buncoed! (By the way,
    �Co.� is Jealousy.)
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ellis Parker Butler, titled "Partners", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
